narrative: OMX30 opens to broad European strength, with DAX and CAC leading. The index sits firmly in bull market structure (+8.2% above 200-MA) and MACD remains above signal, though ADX weakness at 6.1 signals a lack of strong directional commitment. The +0.514% correlation signal and positive risk sentiment from Asia (Hang Seng +2.52%) provide tailwind. However, rising US yields (10Y +49bp to 4.475%) and elevated VIX (+0.81%) inject caution into tech and real estate. USD/SEK at 9.293 (up 0.58%) favours exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson), while commodity strength (gold +1.78%, copper +1.51%) underpins Boliden. The geopolitical headline on US nuclear deployments in Europe adds background risk; watch for any escalation in market sentiment shifts.
morningBrief: OMX30 expected to open +0.4% on continued European rally and positive global correlation signal. Key support at 3085 (lower Bollinger), pivot at 3123 (yesterday close), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). ADX at 6.1 is weak — treat any bullish signals with caution and reduce position sizing by ~30%. Industrials and mining (Atlas Copco, Volvo B, Boliden) ride USD/SEK strength and commodity tailwinds; banks (SEB A, Handelsbanken A) benefit from higher yields widening NIMs. Tech and real estate remain headwinds from yield creep and weak ADX trend. Watch US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for volatility triggers. Risk: geopolitical escalation could trigger sharp VIX spike and unwind risk trades.
drivers: Strong continental European momentum (DAX +0.81%, CAC +0.56%) with positive correlation signal at +0.514%
OMX30 in bull market regime, +8.2% above 200-MA with MACD above signal line
USD/SEK strength (+0.58%) supports industrials and exporters; gold (+1.78%) and copper (+1.51%) lift mining complex
Weak ADX (6.1) limits conviction; RSI 63.22 neutral, no overbought mean-reversion risk

sectorImpacts.reason: US 10Y yield +49bp to 4.475% expands net interest margin expectations for Swedish banks.
USD/SEK strength (+0.58% to 9.293) boosts export pricing power; DAX momentum spills into Swedish cyclicals.
Gold +1.78% and copper +1.51% drive direct revenue and margin lift for Boliden; positive momentum in commodities complex.
US yields rising (+49bp) and NASDAQ underperformance relative to DAX compress tech multiples. Weak ADX limits conviction.
Rising US 10Y yields increase refinancing costs; weak ADX reduces trend support.
Brent oil down 1.37% provides relief, but weak EUR/SEK (up 0.52%) increases H&M sourcing costs. VIX spike pressures discretionary sentiment.

watchList.reason: Gold +1.78% and copper +1.51% are direct revenue drivers; strong momentum in commodities complex today.
USD/SEK +0.58% improves export competitiveness; DAX strength signals industrial momentum.
Strong USD/SEK tailwind and continental European industrial strength; core holding in industrials exposure.
US 10Y +49bp provides NIM expansion; banks outperform in rising-rate environment.
Yields rising compress tech multiples; NASDAQ +0.42% provides minimal lift. Weak ADX limits upside conviction.
Weak EUR/SEK (up 0.52% to 10.82) increases sourcing costs; oil down helps. Conflicting signals.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish releases today. US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET — potential volatility trigger if data surprises; recent labour market softness expected to weigh on risk sentiment if claims rise.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (US nuclear deployments in Europe headline) could trigger sharp VIX spike and rapid unwind of risk trades. ADX weakness at 6.1 means trend support is fragile; any sentiment shift could accelerate downside.
technicalSummary: ADX 6.1 weak — reduce confidence and position sizing by 30%
52-week position 89% of range, -2.7% from high (room to run, but erosion from peak)
